KALIMPONG 40 km — Cycle & Trail Atlas

Every mapped road, jeep track and walking trail within 40 km of Kalimpong town, from real OpenStreetMap survey data — 19,907 ways, 13,971 km of network, 73 marked viewpoints. Hover/tap anything for its name, surface and length. Use the ruler (⌁) to tap out a route and read its exact distance. ★ = viewpoint, ▲ = peak, ⛽ = fuel.

route: 0.0 km

Layers (click to filter)

Ride room

Signature outings

~14 km

CYCLE · Twin Hills Town Loop

Kalimpong bazaar → Deolo Hill (1,704 m, ★ 360° terrace) → Dr Graham's Homes → bazaar → Durpin Hill (★ Zang Dhok Palri monastery, Teesta views). Steep tarmac, big reward.

~26 km loop

CYCLE · Silk-Route Villages Balcony

Kalimpong → Algarah road → Icchey Gaon → Ramdhura (★ Jhandi Dara sunrise point) → Sillery Gaon (★ Kanchenjunga frame) → back via Pedong road. Mix of tarmac + jeep track.

~35 km one-way

CYCLE · Lava Forest Climb

Kalimpong → Algarah → Lava (2,130 m) through Neora fringe forest. Add Rishop by jeep track (+4 km) for the ★ Tiffin Dara ridge. Return same road or drop to Loleygaon.

~4 km walk

WALK · Rishop–Tiffin Dara Trail

Forest footpath from Rishop hamlet to Tiffin Dara ★ — the classic Kanchenjunga dawn walk. Dotted purple on the map; carry water, leeches in monsoon.

~9 km walk

WALK · Sillery Gaon → Ramitey Dara

Ridge trail to Ramitey ★ — the longest visible bend of the Teesta anywhere. Continue toward Damsang Fort ruins on the jeep track if legs allow.

~12 km walk

WALK · Loleygaon Canopy & Kafer Ridge

Heritage Forest canopy walkway → Kafer village trails → Jhandi Dara sunrise point ★. Gentle grades, deep shade — best easy trail day in the belt.

Ground rules

Trails are dotted purple; jeep tracks dashed brown. A trail on the map is a mapped footpath, not a maintained one — condition varies by season. Ask in the last village; most trails here pass homesteads where directions are freely given.
Measure before you commit: tap ⌁ then tap along your intended line — the running km total shows top-right. Hill rule of thumb: cycling 10 km with 500 m gain ≈ 1 hr; walking 4 km ≈ 1 hr + 10 min per 100 m up.
Viewpoint etiquette & safety: dawn is the Kanchenjunga window (clouds by 9 am most months). Carry a headlamp for pre-dawn starts, tell your homestay the route, and expect zero mobile signal in Neora valley folds.
Road & trail geometry © OpenStreetMap contributors (ODbL), fetched 2026-07-04, simplified for display · lengths computed from geometry (±5%) · viewpoints from OSM survey tags · signature-outing distances approximate — verify locally · companion map: Sikkim & Kalimpong Biker's Atlas
